file_insights.pattern: Enum-based command structure with CLI argument attributes
observations: Status command previously had minimal structure (only id argument)
Clap attributes (#[arg]) control CLI parsing with short/long flag variants
Default value for id field suggests optional/fallback behavior
Match statement destructuring pattern mirrors enum field structure

patterns_discovered.name: Clap Derive CLI Patterns
patterns_discovered.description: Use of #[arg(short, long)] attributes for boolean flags with variants

patterns_discovered.name: Command Enum Destructuring
patterns_discovered.description: Pattern matching on enum variants with field destructuring in match arms
patterns_discovered.benefit: Ensures all command variants are handled and new fields propagate to handlers

patterns_discovered.name: Optional ID with Empty Default
patterns_discovered.description: Commands accept optional string IDs with #[arg(default_value = "")] for catch-all behavior
patterns_discovered.usage: Allows conditional routing in handler (empty ID triggers summary, otherwise specific card)

gotchas_discovered.description: Flag signature added but actual watch-mode logic not visible in this diff; handler may be stub or implemented elsewhere
gotchas_discovered.impact: Functionality won't work if handler doesn't use the watch variable

gotchas_discovered.description: Every new field added to enum variant requires match arm update, or code won't compile
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Rust compiler catches this at compile time

approach_outcome.method: Incremental flag addition via Clap derives
steps_taken: Added boolean field `watch: bool` to Status struct variant
Applied clap attributes #[arg(short, long)] for CLI parsing
Updated match arm to destructure new variable
Added doc comment for CLI help text
approach_outcome.verification: Code compiles; flag is wired into argument parser

recommendations.action: Verify the Status command handler actually implements watch-mode behavior using the watch variable
recommendations.rationale: Diff shows signature change only; check that polling/callback logic exists in handler body

recommendations.action: Add integration test for --watch flag: `bop status --watch <card-id>` or `bop status -w <id>`
recommendations.rationale: New CLI flags should have coverage in dispatcher harness or CLI tests

recommendations.action: Expand doc comment to clarify watch behavior (poll interval, output format, exit condition)
recommendations.rationale: Current comment is minimal; users need to understand what 'watch' does

recommendations.action: Check if other commands (Validate, etc.) should also support --watch flag for consistency
recommendations.rationale: Watch mode may be a useful pattern across multiple status/monitoring commands
